name: Health & Wellness
description: A holistic course exploring the multiple dimensions of wellness — physical, mental, emotional, social, and environmental — and strategies for creating a balanced, healthy lifestyle.

modules.title: Dimensions of Wellness
modules.objectives: Identify the seven dimensions of wellness (physical, emotional, intellectual, social, spiritual, environmental, occupational) and explain how they interconnect.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Health and medicine lesson videos for this topic: Dimensions of Wellness.
modules.practiceNote: Complete a wellness wheel self-assessment to identify your strongest and weakest dimensions
modules.assignment: Complete a personal wellness wheel assessment, rate yourself in each dimension (1–10), and write a 1-page reflection identifying your top two areas for growth and one action step for each.

modules.title: Physical Wellness & Exercise
modules.objectives: Explain how regular physical activity contributes to overall wellness, understand physical activity guidelines for adolescents, and identify barriers to exercise.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Health and medicine lesson videos for this topic: Physical Wellness & Exercise.
modules.practiceNote: Track your daily physical activity for one week and compare to the recommended 60 minutes per day
modules.assignment: Create a 2-week physical activity log tracking type, duration, and intensity of activity. Reflect on how physical activity impacted your mood and energy each day.

modules.title: Nutritional Wellness
modules.objectives: Apply MyPlate guidelines to evaluate and improve daily food choices, understand how nutrition affects energy, mood, and long-term health.
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How Diet Affects Mental and Physical Health
modules.practiceNote: Use the MyPlate Daily Checklist to plan and evaluate your meals for one day
modules.assignment: Track your food intake for 3 days using MyPlate, then write a reflection on how your eating habits align with nutritional wellness and identify two specific improvements.

modules.title: Mental & Emotional Health
modules.objectives: Define mental and emotional health, identify common mental health conditions in adolescents, and describe strategies for maintaining positive mental health.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Health and medicine lesson videos for this topic: Mental & Emotional Health.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the NIMH teen mental health self-check and identify any concerns to discuss with a counselor
modules.assignment: Research and write a 1-page guide on two mental health strategies for adolescents (e.g., journaling, mindfulness), explaining the evidence for each and how to practice them.

modules.title: Stress Management
modules.objectives: Identify common stressors for teenagers, explain the physiological stress response, and apply multiple evidence-based stress management techniques.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Fight or Flight Response Explained
modules.practiceNote: Identify your top 3 stressors and practice one stress management technique for 5 days
modules.assignment: Create a personal stress management toolkit identifying your top three stressors and three evidence-based coping strategies with a plan for implementing each one.

modules.title: Sleep & Recovery
modules.objectives: Explain the stages of sleep, understand the recommended sleep duration for teenagers, and identify habits that promote and disrupt healthy sleep.
modules.videoNote: YouTube — Why Teens Need More Sleep
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Sleep Stages and Their Importance
modules.practiceNote: Track your sleep schedule for one week and evaluate your sleep quality using a sleep diary
modules.assignment: Track your sleep for 5 nights recording bedtime, wake time, and sleep quality. Identify 3 habits harming your sleep and write a 2-week sleep improvement plan.

modules.title: Social Health & Relationships
modules.objectives: Define social health, identify characteristics of healthy vs. unhealthy relationships, and understand effective communication skills for building positive connections.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Health and medicine lesson videos for this topic: Social Health & Relationships.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the relationship health quiz at loveisrespect.org
modules.assignment: Write a personal reflection on the qualities you value in friendships and family relationships, describing one communication skill you want to develop and how you will practice it.

modules.title: Substance Use & Prevention
modules.objectives: Identify the short- and long-term health effects of tobacco, alcohol, and other substances on the developing brain, and apply refusal skills and protective strategies.
modules.readingNote: CDC — Alcohol Use Facts and Prevention
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Health and medicine lesson videos for this topic: Substance Use & Prevention.
modules.practiceNote: Research the CDC's statistics on teen substance use and identify three prevention strategies
modules.assignment: Create a prevention resource for peers that explains the brain effects of one substance (alcohol, nicotine, or cannabis), risk factors for addiction, and three refusal skill strategies.

quizQuestions.question: How many dimensions of wellness does SAMHSA identify?
quizQuestions.explanation: SAMHSA identifies 8 dimensions of wellness: emotional, financial, social, spiritual, occupational, physical, intellectual, and environmental.

quizQuestions.question: Which best describes the concept of 'holistic wellness'?
options: Recognizing that all wellness dimensions are interconnected and equally important
Prioritizing spiritual health above all others
Achieving perfect health in every dimension
quizQuestions.answer: Recognizing that all wellness dimensions are interconnected and equally important
quizQuestions.explanation: Holistic wellness acknowledges that all health dimensions are interconnected — weakness in one can affect all others.

quizQuestions.question: How many dimensions of wellness does SAMHSA identify in its wellness model?
quizQuestions.explanation: SAMHSA commonly presents an eight-dimension wellness model.

quizQuestions.question: Short response: Define or explain: Occupational wellness refers to.
quizQuestions.answer: Occupational wellness refers to finding healthy balance, purpose, growth, and satisfaction in school, work, responsibilities, and future career preparation.
quizQuestions.explanation: The concept is not only about having a job; for students it includes using strengths, managing responsibilities, and building a sustainable path.

quizQuestions.question: How much daily physical activity do adolescents need according to health guidelines?
quizQuestions.answer: 60 minutes per day
quizQuestions.explanation: The CDC recommends that adolescents get at least 60 minutes of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity every day.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of physical activity is recommended at least 3 days per week for teens?
quizQuestions.answer: Muscle-strengthening activities
quizQuestions.explanation: Muscle and bone-strengthening activities (resistance training, gymnastics) are recommended at least 3 days per week for teens.

quizQuestions.question: A common barrier to exercise for teenagers is:
options: Lack of time due to academic and social commitments
quizQuestions.answer: Lack of time due to academic and social commitments
quizQuestions.explanation: Time constraints from school, homework, and social activities are among the most frequently cited barriers to teen physical activity.

quizQuestions.question: According to youth physical activity guidelines, adolescents should aim for about how much daily physical activity?
options: Three hours every day without rest
quizQuestions.answer: 60 minutes per day
quizQuestions.explanation: Health education guidelines commonly recommend about 60 minutes of daily physical activity for adolescents.

quizQuestions.question: MyPlate recommends that half your plate be filled with:
quizQuestions.answer: Fruits and vegetables
quizQuestions.explanation: The USDA MyPlate guideline recommends filling half of every meal's plate with fruits and vegetables.

quizQuestions.question: How does poor nutrition most directly affect mental wellness?
options: It has no connection to mental health
Nutrient deficiencies can impair brain function, mood, and cognitive performance
quizQuestions.answer: Nutrient deficiencies can impair brain function, mood, and cognitive performance
quizQuestions.explanation: Nutritional deficiencies (e.g., iron, B vitamins, omega-3s) can negatively impact brain chemistry, mood regulation, and cognitive function.

quizQuestions.question: Which eating habit best supports nutritional wellness?
options: Eating a variety of whole, minimally processed foods from all food groups
quizQuestions.answer: Eating a variety of whole, minimally processed foods from all food groups
quizQuestions.explanation: Nutritional wellness is supported by variety, balance, and adequacy — choosing whole foods from all food groups in appropriate amounts.

quizQuestions.question: Short response: Define or explain: Hydration's impact on wellness includes.
quizQuestions.answer: Hydration supports wellness by helping regulate body temperature, move nutrients, support digestion, maintain energy, and protect concentration and physical performance.
quizQuestions.explanation: Water affects multiple body systems, so a complete answer should connect hydration to both physical and mental performance.

quizQuestions.question: Mental health is best defined as:
options: The absence of all mental illness
Emotional, psychological, and social well-being that affects thinking, feeling, and behavior
quizQuestions.answer: Emotional, psychological, and social well-being that affects thinking, feeling, and behavior
quizQuestions.explanation: Mental health encompasses emotional, psychological, and social well-being, affecting how we think, feel, and interact with the world.

quizQuestions.question: Anxiety disorders are the most common mental health condition in:
quizQuestions.answer: Teenagers and young adults
quizQuestions.explanation: Anxiety disorders are the most prevalent mental health conditions in adolescents and young adults, often beginning in childhood or teen years.

quizQuestions.question: Which is NOT a healthy coping strategy for mental health challenges?
options: Using alcohol to reduce feelings of anxiety
quizQuestions.answer: Using alcohol to reduce feelings of anxiety
quizQuestions.explanation: Alcohol is an unhealthy and potentially harmful coping mechanism that masks symptoms rather than addressing mental health challenges.

quizQuestions.question: Stigma around mental health prevents many teens from seeking help because:
options: Mental health treatment is illegal for teens
They fear judgment, social exclusion, or being labeled as weak
Therapy is too expensive for all teens
Mental illness is not taken seriously by doctors
quizQuestions.answer: They fear judgment, social exclusion, or being labeled as weak
quizQuestions.explanation: Social stigma — fear of being judged, labeled, or excluded — is one of the primary barriers preventing teens from seeking mental health support.

quizQuestions.question: The body's 'fight or flight' response is triggered by which hormone?
quizQuestions.answer: Cortisol and adrenaline (epinephrine)
quizQuestions.explanation: The fight-or-flight response involves the release of cortisol and adrenaline from the adrenal glands in response to perceived threats.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an evidence-based stress management technique?
options: Deep breathing exercises and mindfulness meditation
quizQuestions.answer: Deep breathing exercises and mindfulness meditation
quizQuestions.explanation: Deep breathing and mindfulness meditation activate the parasympathetic nervous system, reducing physiological stress responses.

quizQuestions.question: Which hormones are commonly associated with the body's fight-or-flight stress response?
quizQuestions.answer: Adrenaline (epinephrine) and cortisol
quizQuestions.explanation: The stress response involves hormones such as adrenaline and cortisol.

quizQuestions.question: Which option is an evidence-informed stress management technique?
options: Deep breathing exercises or mindfulness practice
Avoiding sleep to get more done
quizQuestions.answer: Deep breathing exercises or mindfulness practice
quizQuestions.explanation: Breathing exercises and mindfulness are commonly taught coping strategies; rumination tends to maintain stress.

quizQuestions.question: How many hours of sleep per night do teenagers need?
quizQuestions.answer: 8–10 hours
quizQuestions.explanation: The CDC and American Academy of Sleep Medicine recommend 8–10 hours of sleep per night for teenagers aged 13–18.

quizQuestions.question: REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep is especially important for:
quizQuestions.answer: Memory consolidation and emotional processing
quizQuestions.explanation: REM sleep plays a critical role in consolidating memories, processing emotions, and supporting learning and cognitive function.

quizQuestions.question: Blue light from screens before bed disrupts sleep by:
options: Suppressing melatonin production and delaying sleep onset
quizQuestions.answer: Suppressing melatonin production and delaying sleep onset
quizQuestions.explanation: Blue light mimics daylight, suppressing melatonin (the sleep hormone), making it harder to fall asleep at a natural bedtime.

quizQuestions.question: How many hours of sleep per night are generally recommended for teenagers?
quizQuestions.answer: 8-10 hours
quizQuestions.explanation: Sleep education guidelines commonly recommend about 8-10 hours for teens.

quizQuestions.question: Short response: Define or explain: Sleep deprivation in teens is associated with.
quizQuestions.answer: Sleep deprivation in teens is associated with poorer attention and learning, mood problems, higher injury risk, and increased risk of several health concerns.
quizQuestions.explanation: A strong answer recognizes sleep as a health behavior that affects school performance, emotional regulation, safety, and long-term wellness.

quizQuestions.question: A healthy relationship is characterized by:
options: Mutual respect, trust, honesty, and support
quizQuestions.answer: Mutual respect, trust, honesty, and support
quizQuestions.explanation: Healthy relationships are built on mutual respect, trust, open communication, honesty, and support for each other's wellbeing.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a warning sign of an unhealthy relationship?
options: One partner attempting to control the other's behavior
quizQuestions.answer: One partner attempting to control the other's behavior
quizQuestions.explanation: Controlling behavior, including monitoring movements, isolating from friends, or making decisions for the partner, is a warning sign of abuse.

quizQuestions.question: Which qualities best characterize a healthy relationship?
options: Mutual respect, trust, honesty, and support
quizQuestions.answer: Mutual respect, trust, honesty, and support
quizQuestions.explanation: Healthy relationships are built on respect, trust, honesty, support, and boundaries.

quizQuestions.question: Short response: Define or explain: Active listening involves.
quizQuestions.answer: Active listening involves giving full attention, using appropriate nonverbal cues, asking clarifying questions, paraphrasing the speaker's meaning, and avoiding interrupting.
quizQuestions.explanation: The goal is to show understanding and respect, not simply to stay silent while waiting to respond.

quizQuestions.question: Nicotine in tobacco products affects the developing teen brain by:
options: Having no effect on teenage brains
Disrupting formation of brain circuits that control attention, learning, and impulse control
quizQuestions.answer: Disrupting formation of brain circuits that control attention, learning, and impulse control
quizQuestions.explanation: Nicotine is highly addictive and disrupts developing brain circuits responsible for attention, mood regulation, learning, and impulse control in teens.

quizQuestions.question: The minimum legal drinking age in the United States is:
quizQuestions.explanation: The legal drinking age in all 50 US states is 21 years old.

quizQuestions.question: Which factor most protects teens from substance use?
options: Strong family bonds and healthy peer relationships
quizQuestions.answer: Strong family bonds and healthy peer relationships
quizQuestions.explanation: Strong family connections, positive peer relationships, and school engagement are among the strongest protective factors against teen substance use.

quizQuestions.question: In health education, how is nicotine commonly described as affecting the developing teen brain?
options: It can disrupt brain circuits related to attention, learning, and impulse control
It improves long-term attention and learning
It has no effect on adolescent development
It replaces the need for sleep
quizQuestions.answer: It can disrupt brain circuits related to attention, learning, and impulse control
quizQuestions.explanation: Nicotine exposure during adolescence is taught as a risk to brain development and addiction vulnerability.

questions.question: SAMHSA's model identifies how many dimensions of wellness?
questions.explanation: SAMHSA identifies 8 dimensions: emotional, financial, social, spiritual, occupational, physical, intellectual, and environmental.

questions.question: The CDC recommends how many minutes of daily physical activity for teenagers?
questions.answer: 60 minutes
questions.explanation: The CDC recommends at least 60 minutes of moderate-to-vigorous physical activity daily for adolescents.

questions.question: Mental health is best described as:
options: The absence of any mental disorder
questions.answer: Emotional, psychological, and social well-being
questions.explanation: Mental health is a positive concept encompassing emotional, psychological, and social well-being, not just the absence of illness.

questions.question: The fight-or-flight stress response involves the release of which hormones?
questions.answer: Adrenaline (epinephrine) and cortisol
questions.explanation: Adrenaline and cortisol help prepare the body for a perceived threat.

questions.question: What sleep range is generally recommended for teenagers each night?
questions.answer: 8–10 hours
questions.explanation: The American Academy of Sleep Medicine recommends 8–10 hours of sleep per night for teenagers aged 13–18.

questions.question: Blue light from screens disrupts sleep by:
options: Suppressing melatonin and delaying sleep onset
questions.answer: Suppressing melatonin and delaying sleep onset
questions.explanation: Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin production, making it harder to fall asleep at the normal bedtime.

questions.question: Which qualities should be present in a healthy relationship?
questions.answer: A healthy relationship is characterized by respect, trust, honest communication, boundaries, support, safety, and shared responsibility.
questions.explanation: The answer should include both positive communication and safety boundaries, because healthy relationships require more than simply spending time together.

questions.question: MyPlate recommends filling about how much of the plate with fruits and vegetables?
options: The entire plate with fruit only
questions.answer: One half
questions.explanation: MyPlate visually emphasizes fruits and vegetables as about half the plate.

questions.question: Which substance is both addictive and harmful to the developing teen brain?
options: Caffeine in moderate amounts from natural sources
questions.answer: Nicotine from e-cigarettes and tobacco
questions.explanation: Nicotine is highly addictive and disrupts the developing adolescent brain's circuits for attention, learning, and impulse control.

questions.question: Which is a warning sign of an unhealthy relationship?
options: Controlling and isolating behavior by one partner
questions.answer: Controlling and isolating behavior by one partner
questions.explanation: Controlling, isolating, or monitoring a partner's behavior are red flags for emotional or physical abuse in a relationship.

questions.question: Eustress differs from distress because eustress:
options: Is positive, motivating stress that improves performance
questions.answer: Is positive, motivating stress that improves performance
questions.explanation: Eustress is positive, energizing stress that motivates and enhances performance, while distress is negative and can harm health.

questions.question: In sexual health education, consent requires agreement that is best described as:
options: Freely given, informed, specific, reversible, and enthusiastic
Valid when pressured by a partner
questions.answer: Freely given, informed, specific, reversible, and enthusiastic
questions.explanation: Consent must be active, informed, voluntary, specific, and can be withdrawn.

questions.question: Chronic stress can harm physical health by:
options: Elevating cortisol, suppressing immunity, and increasing heart disease risk
questions.answer: Elevating cortisol, suppressing immunity, and increasing heart disease risk
questions.explanation: Sustained cortisol elevation from chronic stress weakens the immune system, raises blood pressure, and increases chronic disease risk.

questions.question: REM sleep is especially important for:
questions.answer: REM sleep is especially important for dreaming, emotional processing, memory consolidation, and healthy brain function.
questions.explanation: REM is one stage in a full sleep cycle, and the model answer should connect it to mental and cognitive recovery.

questions.question: Which protective factor can reduce teen substance-use risk?
options: Strong family bonds and positive peer relationships
questions.answer: Strong family bonds and positive peer relationships
questions.explanation: Strong family connections, caring adults, and positive peer relationships are among the strongest protective factors against adolescent substance use.

questions.question: Social determinants of health include which kinds of factors?
options: Conditions where people are born, live, learn, work, and access resources
questions.answer: Conditions where people are born, live, learn, work, and access resources
questions.explanation: Social determinants are social and environmental conditions that shape health opportunities.

questions.question: The leading cause of death in the United States is:
questions.answer: Heart disease
questions.explanation: Heart disease has been the leading cause of death in the US for decades, accounting for approximately 1 in 4 deaths annually.

questions.question: A credible health information website is most likely to have which feature?
options: Cited authors, peer-reviewed sources, and a .gov or .edu domain
questions.answer: Cited authors, peer-reviewed sources, and a .gov or .edu domain
questions.explanation: Credible health sites provide clear author credentials, citations from peer-reviewed sources, and are often associated with government or educational institutions.

questions.question: HPV is unique among STIs because:
options: A preventive vaccine (Gardasil) exists and is recommended for teens
questions.answer: A preventive vaccine (Gardasil) exists and is recommended for teens
questions.explanation: HPV is the only STI for which a highly effective preventive vaccine is available and recommended for preteens and teenagers.

questions.question: A wellness plan is most effective when it does what?
options: Uses realistic goals, clear action steps, and progress checks across multiple wellness dimensions
Focuses on one extreme goal with no review
questions.answer: Uses realistic goals, clear action steps, and progress checks across multiple wellness dimensions
questions.explanation: A useful wellness plan is realistic, measurable, and balanced across wellness dimensions.

questions.question: The Air Quality Index (AQI) is a measure of:
questions.answer: The Air Quality Index is a measure of how polluted the outdoor air is and what level of health concern that pollution may pose.
questions.explanation: AQI translates pollutant levels into a public health scale so people can decide whether to adjust outdoor activity.

questions.question: Health equity means:
options: All people have a fair opportunity to achieve their highest possible health
Only the wealthy deserve health resources
Health outcomes are determined solely by genetics
questions.answer: All people have a fair opportunity to achieve their highest possible health
questions.explanation: Health equity means removing barriers so everyone has a fair opportunity to achieve optimal health, regardless of social circumstances.

questions.question: Preventive healthcare includes:
options: Routine checkups, vaccinations, and health screenings
questions.answer: Routine checkups, vaccinations, and health screenings
questions.explanation: Preventive care detects risk factors and conditions early through regular checkups, vaccinations, and recommended health screenings.

questions.question: Physical inactivity is classified as which type of chronic disease risk factor?
options: Modifiable through behavior and environmental support
Non-modifiable and determined only by genetics
questions.answer: Modifiable through behavior and environmental support
questions.explanation: Physical activity patterns can often be changed with planning, support, and safer access to activity.

questions.question: WHO defines health as:
options: Absence of disease or infirmity only
A complete state of physical, mental, and social well-being
questions.answer: A complete state of physical, mental, and social well-being
questions.explanation: The World Health Organization defines health as a state of complete physical, mental, and social well-being, not merely the absence of disease.

questions.question: Which sleep habit is most effective for improving sleep quality?
options: Varying sleep and wake times daily
questions.answer: Maintaining a consistent sleep schedule
questions.explanation: A consistent sleep-wake schedule strengthens the circadian rhythm and is the most evidence-based sleep hygiene practice.

questions.question: Active listening improves relationships because it:
options: Allows you to prepare your response while someone talks
Demonstrates full attention and understanding, reducing conflict
Requires you to talk more than listen
Is only used in professional settings
questions.answer: Demonstrates full attention and understanding, reducing conflict
questions.explanation: Active listening — giving full attention and reflecting back what's heard — reduces misunderstandings and strengthens relationship trust.

questions.question: E-cigarettes are harmful to teens because they contain:
questions.answer: E-cigarettes are harmful to teens because many contain nicotine, which is addictive and can harm adolescent brain development; aerosols may also contain other harmful substances.
questions.explanation: A strong answer should not treat vaping as harmless. Nicotine addiction and effects on the developing brain are the central teen-health concerns.

questions.question: Type 2 diabetes is most linked to which modifiable lifestyle factors?
options: Too much sleep and vitamin excess
Excessive exercise and high protein intake
questions.answer: Obesity and physical inactivity
questions.explanation: Obesity and physical inactivity are the primary modifiable risk factors for type 2 diabetes, making it largely preventable.

questions.question: A SMART wellness goal must be:
options: Vague and flexible to allow any outcome
Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound
Set by a healthcare provider only
questions.answer: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound
questions.explanation: SMART goals provide clear, actionable targets with specific criteria that make progress measurable and achievement realistic.

questions.question: Occupational wellness in a high school student most directly relates to:
options: Finding meaning and purpose in academic work and extracurricular activities
questions.answer: Finding meaning and purpose in academic work and extracurricular activities
questions.explanation: For students, occupational wellness means finding satisfaction, purpose, and engagement in academic work and activities.

questions.question: Waterborne diseases are caused by what?
options: Pathogens or contaminants in unsafe water
questions.answer: Pathogens or contaminants in unsafe water
questions.explanation: Waterborne disease risk comes from contaminated water, not properly treated clean water.

questions.question: The most effective approach to stress management includes:
options: Using a variety of healthy coping strategies tailored to the stressor type
Relying on substances to reduce stress
questions.answer: Using a variety of healthy coping strategies tailored to the stressor type
questions.explanation: Using diverse, healthy coping strategies — exercise, social support, mindfulness, time management — matched to different stressors is most effective.

questions.question: Mindful eating involves:
options: Paying full attention to hunger, flavors, and eating without distraction
questions.answer: Paying full attention to hunger, flavors, and eating without distraction
questions.explanation: Mindful eating means eating with full awareness of hunger and satiety cues, flavors, and the eating experience without distraction.

questions.question: Tracking progress in a personal wellness plan is important because it:
questions.answer: Tracking progress in a personal wellness plan is important because it shows patterns, measures whether goals are realistic, supports accountability, and helps people adjust habits over time.
questions.explanation: Wellness plans work best when students use evidence from their own behavior to revise goals instead of relying on vague intentions.
